title: External Debt Repayments

fy26.label: FY2025-26
fy26.note: Pakistan owes about $25.9bn in external debt servicing in FY2025-26. Roughly $16bn is expected to be rolled over by friendly creditors (China, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait), leaving about $10bn of hard cash outflow (~$6bn interest + ~$4bn principal). Around $9bn of the servicing sits on the SBP's books.

stock.note: Total external debt & liabilities reached about $134.5bn by Q1 FY2026. Outstanding credit to the IMF was SDR 7.1bn (~$9.3bn) at end-March 2026.

repaymentSplit.label: Expected rollovers
repaymentSplit.note: Re-financed / rolled over with creditors' consent.

repaymentSplit.label: Interest (hard cash)
repaymentSplit.note: Interest on debt not rolled over.

repaymentSplit.label: Principal (hard cash)
repaymentSplit.note: Principal not expected to be rolled over.

fy27.label: FY2026-27
fy27.note: Detailed FY2026-27 maturities are not yet fully public, but obligations are expected to remain at a similar scale ($24–26bn), keeping rollover support critical.

riskNote: Pakistan's external position depends heavily on rollovers of bilateral deposits and loans. Any failure to secure rollovers would sharply increase the hard-cash repayment burden and pressure reserves.
methodologyNote: External debt servicing figures are gross obligations for the fiscal year, compiled from SBP briefings and Pakistani financial media. The rollover vs hard-cash split is an estimate disclosed to analysts (with the IMF present) and is subject to change as rollovers are secured.
